proof_boundary: This suite creates a top-10 benchmark register, abstained app snapshot placeholders, trivial prior controls, public baseline provider map, and scorecard scaffold. It does not prove prediction accuracy, top-three status, hosted behavior, buyer validation, or benchmark superiority.

public_baseline_fetchers.probability_field_policy: Use submitted model probability and Brier Index only when question mapping is accepted by evaluator.
public_baseline_fetchers.question_mapping_policy: Same question or documented comparable ForecastBench market/dataset question only.
public_baseline_fetchers.claim_boundary: Methodology and ranking anchor, not automatic same-question baseline.

public_baseline_fetchers.probability_field_policy: Use bot, community, or pro forecast only when the same Metaculus question is mapped and timestamped.
public_baseline_fetchers.question_mapping_policy: Same Metaculus question preferred; comparable question requires reviewer approval.
public_baseline_fetchers.claim_boundary: Compare only on shared or documented comparable resolved questions.

public_baseline_fetchers.probability_field_policy: Use midpoint of best bid/ask when spread is narrow; otherwise use last traded price with spread caveat.
public_baseline_fetchers.question_mapping_policy: Mapped market resolution language must match the benchmark question or be excluded.
public_baseline_fetchers.claim_boundary: Market-implied probability, not human/pro forecaster proof.

public_baseline_fetchers.probability_field_policy: Use public market probability only when market resolution criteria match.
public_baseline_fetchers.question_mapping_policy: Mapped market must be binary and same-direction after fee/currency caveats are reviewed.
public_baseline_fetchers.claim_boundary: Community-market baseline with social-market liquidity caveats.

public_baseline_fetchers.probability_field_policy: Use market price as implied probability after bid/ask and fee caveats are captured.
public_baseline_fetchers.question_mapping_policy: Mapped event contract must match resolution criteria and time boundary.
public_baseline_fetchers.claim_boundary: Regulated market baseline where matching markets exist.

challenger_lanes.label: Current app registry and multi-agent role-weighted forecast
challenger_lanes.mechanism: Use existing forecast registry, consensus, and calibration governance.
challenger_lanes.promotion_gate: Default lane until a challenger wins on resolved same-question rows.

challenger_lanes.label: AIA-style LLM forecaster
challenger_lanes.mechanism: Retrieval, base rates, decomposition, premortem, supervisor reconciliation, and statistical calibration.
challenger_lanes.promotion_gate: Shadow-only until leakage review passes and holdout Brier/log loss beats current app lane.

challenger_lanes.label: Public market consensus
challenger_lanes.mechanism: Use Polymarket, Manifold, and Kalshi implied probabilities when liquid and comparable.
challenger_lanes.promotion_gate: Eligible as ensemble feature only; never a standalone world-class proof.

challenger_lanes.label: Macro, market, energy, and commodity time-series forecaster
challenger_lanes.mechanism: Use Nixtla/StatsForecast-style probabilistic horizons for structured time-series questions.
challenger_lanes.promotion_gate: Only for source-backed numeric or threshold questions with clean historical series.

challenger_lanes.label: Calibration and meta-ensemble
challenger_lanes.mechanism: Use sigmoid/isotonic calibration, then stacking only after enough resolved rows.
challenger_lanes.promotion_gate: No stacking before minimum resolved samples; per-domain calibration must be reported.

challenger_lanes.label: Online drift monitoring
challenger_lanes.mechanism: Track changing evidence distributions and forecast-age risk.
challenger_lanes.promotion_gate: Alerting lane, not direct probability source.

promotion_rules.allowed_claim: internal pilot calibration report on documented resolved rows
minimum_conditions: at least 25 resolved same-question rows for one source
Brier score and reliability bins reported

promotion_rules.allowed_claim: candidate for top-three web-app forecasting review
minimum_conditions: ranked top 3 by Brier or log-skill against chosen public/open pool
independent benchmark review or accepted external benchmark

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: Major conflict escalation monitor
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will the CFR Global Conflict Tracker classify at least one monitored conflict as worsening by 2026-09-30?
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if the CFR Global Conflict Tracker lists any monitored conflict with a worsening status on or before 2026-09-30 compared with the 2026-06-07 benchmark packet review date; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: If CFR changes labels or the tracker is unavailable, use archived CFR pages and reviewer notes; unresolved label drift stays excluded.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude if the source retires the tracker or no archived status comparison can be established.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Stress-tests geopolitical escalation reasoning for strategic risk buyers.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: G7 leadership change outcome
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will any G7 country install a new head of government through a national election or parliamentary confidence process by 2026-12-31?
forecast_benchmark_questions.source_surface: IDEA election database plus official government pages
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if an official G7 government source or IDEA election record confirms a new head of government took office through a national election or parliamentary confidence process by 2026-12-31; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: Caretaker, acting, or interim changes count only when the official head-of-government office changes.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude cabinet reshuffles without a head-of-government change.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests political-transition forecasts relevant to country-risk and policy buyers.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: Federal Reserve easing surprise
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will the FOMC target range upper bound be at least 50 basis points lower on 2026-09-30 than the range in force on 2026-06-07?
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if the Federal Reserve implementation note or FOMC statement in force on 2026-09-30 shows an upper bound at least 50 bps below the target range in force on 2026-06-07; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: If the target-rate framework changes, convert to the nearest official target-range upper-bound equivalent by reviewer note.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude if official Federal Reserve target-rate publication is unavailable for the resolution date.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests macro policy prediction under market-sensitive conditions.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: Brent crude upside shock
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will front-month Brent crude settle above USD 95 on any trading day by 2026-12-31?
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if ICE or another reviewer-approved market data source records a front-month Brent crude settlement above USD 95 on any trading day through 2026-12-31; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: If ICE public pages are unavailable, use EIA or CME-compatible public market data with the same front-month contract definition.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude intraday prints without a settlement price.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests energy-shock forecasting for procurement, commodities, and country-risk workflows.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: Gold safe-haven breakout
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will the LBMA PM gold price exceed USD 3800 per troy ounce on any fixing day by 2026-12-31?
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if the LBMA PM gold price exceeds USD 3800 per troy ounce on any official fixing day through 2026-12-31; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: If LBMA data access changes, use a reviewer-approved public mirror that identifies the LBMA PM fixing.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude spot-price quotes that are not the LBMA PM fixing.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests commodity and safe-haven reasoning for inflation and conflict-linked market scenarios.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: S&P 500 drawdown risk
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will the S&P 500 close at least 10 percent below its 2026-06-07 reference close on any trading day by 2026-12-31?
forecast_benchmark_questions.source_surface: S&P Dow Jones Indices closing level
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if S&P Dow Jones Indices or an approved public market-data source records an S&P 500 close at least 10 percent below the closing level used for the 2026-06-07 benchmark packet; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: If index methodology changes, use the official S&P 500 closing level under the prevailing methodology.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude intraday lows, futures, ETFs, or unofficial derived closes.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests cross-asset market shock prediction tied to executive risk decisions.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: US dollar index upside risk
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will the US Dollar Index close above 110 on any trading day by 2026-12-31?
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if ICE or another approved public market-data source records a US Dollar Index closing level above 110 on any trading day through 2026-12-31; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: If public ICE data is unavailable, use a reviewer-approved public source that explicitly tracks DXY closing levels.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude intraday prints, futures-only settlement proxies, or non-DXY dollar baskets.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests FX and sovereign-risk signal fusion for globally exposed buyers.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: Major trade-dispute initiation
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will the WTO publish a new dispute involving the United States, European Union, or China as a complainant or respondent by 2026-12-31?
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if the WTO dispute settlement database publishes a new dispute number by 2026-12-31 where the United States, European Union, or China is a complainant or respondent; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: Use the WTO publication date and named parties; consultation requests count when assigned a WTO dispute number.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude press speculation, domestic cases, or disputes without a WTO dispute number.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests tariff and supply-chain disruption forecasting for corporate strategy buyers.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: Atlantic major hurricane occurrence
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will NOAA report at least one Atlantic named storm reaching Category 4 or 5 intensity by 2026-11-30?
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if NOAA/NHC best-track or public advisory data reports at least one Atlantic named storm reaching Category 4 or 5 intensity during the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season by 2026-11-30; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: Use post-season best-track updates if advisory and best-track intensity conflict.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude non-Atlantic storms and systems without named-storm status.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests disaster-risk forecasting with public scientific data sources.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.

forecast_benchmark_questions.title: EU AI Act high-risk timing change
forecast_benchmark_questions.question_text: Will the EU publish binding law by 2026-12-31 that delays the first application date for EU AI Act high-risk AI obligations?
forecast_benchmark_questions.source_surface: EUR-Lex EU AI Act legal text
forecast_benchmark_questions.resolution_criteria: Resolve yes if EUR-Lex publishes binding EU law by 2026-12-31 that delays the first application date for high-risk AI obligations under Regulation 2024/1689; otherwise resolve no.
forecast_benchmark_questions.ambiguity_policy: Guidance, consultation papers, or non-binding announcements do not count unless followed by binding law.
forecast_benchmark_questions.exclusion_rule: Exclude delays limited to unrelated AI Act provisions.
forecast_benchmark_questions.decision_relevance: Tests AI regulation and compliance-timing forecasts for enterprise and public-sector buyers.
forecast_benchmark_questions.notes: Cross-domain top-10 situation row; score only after frozen probabilities and outcomes exist.
